scope.title: Add native CKC/PoseKit operator and model quickstart coverage
scope.base_scope: Update Handshake's canonical in-app UserManual so a no-context operator or model can discover and use the already-built native Atelier CKC/PoseKit surfaces without treating the original CastKit app as the implementation target.
non_goals: Do not rebuild CKC or PoseKit from the old CastKit app.
Do not edit visual UI files in this MT.
Do not mark planned Pose/Comfy library commands as wired unless a real route exists.
acceptance_criteria: Add a canonical UserManual quickstart page for native Atelier CKC/PoseKit operation.
The page must clearly say CKC/PoseKit are already native Handshake Atelier surfaces.
The page must guide operators/models through image intake, pose rig/sidecar/identity work, ComfyUI receipts/replay, deferred boundaries, and visual polish targets.
The page must link from the manual TOC and quickstart set so it is reachable.
Bump USER_MANUAL_VERSION because seed content changes.
Add a focused test that proves the quickstart page exists and contains the key native guidance terms.

commands_planned: Edit canonical user_manual seed and version.
Run scoped handshake_core user manual content test target or the narrowest compile/test command available.

results: Implemented canonical UserManual quickstart page quickstart-atelier-ckc-posekit and linked it from manual-toc, product-purpose navigation, and QUICKSTART_AREAS.
Initial rustfmt without edition failed because rustfmt defaulted to Rust 2015 on async files; rerun with --edition 2024 succeeded but also formatted child modules.
Accidental rustfmt spillover was reverted for non-target user_manual and support files; final product diff is limited to the three MT target files plus inherited baseline files.
Full user_manual_content_tests cargo run timed out after 300 seconds with no verdict.
Exact single-test cargo run timed out after 600 seconds with no verdict.
Timed-out user_manual_content_tests cargo/rustc process tree was stopped; unrelated handshake-native cargo processes from another workflow were detected and left untouched.
rustfmt --check with skip_children reported existing formatting differences in touched large files and was not used as a PASS verdict.

Focused exact test rerun passed: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --jobs 1 --test user_manual_content_tests ckc_posekit_quickstart_is_native_first_and_reachable -- --exact => 1 passed, 0 failed.
Full target rerun passed: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --jobs 1 --test user_manual_content_tests => 13 passed, 0 failed.

risks.risk: Manual text implies CKC/PoseKit still need to be rebuilt rather than polished.
risks.mitigation: Use explicit wording that the native Handshake surfaces already exist and the old app is behavior reference only.

risks.risk: Manual claims a backend route is wired when it is only a Rust library surface.
risks.mitigation: Do not change ModelManual command statuses; describe planned/library surfaces accurately.

risks.risk: New page is orphaned and invisible to operators/models.
risks.mitigation: Add TOC and quickstart anchors, and cover it with seed reachability tests.

handoff.summary: Manual/discoverability update completed for already-native Handshake Atelier CKC/PoseKit. Focused exact test passed, and the full user_manual_content_tests target passed with 13 passed, 0 failed.
